Open Source Intel NEW 🔴 Following reports of no ships departing Iranian reports, at least 2 ships that departed from Iranian ports transited the Strait of Hormuz, despite the U.S. blockade in the area. The report is based on data from Kpler, which tracks oil data in real time and analyzes vessel movement and commodity flows. One of the ships, sailing under a Liberian flag, crossed the strait after unloading corn at Iran’s Bandar Imam Khomeini port. The second vessel, which also transited the strait, was sailing under a Comoros flag. tweet

Status-6 (War & Military News) On Tuesday, Russian Air Force conducted airstrikes with FAB UMPK guided aerial bombs on the Pechenihy reservoir dam located in Ukraine's Kharkiv Oblast. According to the head of Kharkiv Regional Military Administration, Oleh Syniehubov, the object was also targeted by Russian Molniya and Shahed drones, although these attacks are not seen in the footage. tweet

🇷🇸 🇮🇱 ⚡️ Serbia and Israel to Jointly Produce Combat Drones, Vučić Announces “We will be doing it together… 50-50… and we will have the best drones in this part of the world,” Vučić said during a visit to a military unit. He added that Serbia alone “cannot make drones like Israel.” The project involves Israel’s Elbit Systems (holding a 51% stake) and Serbia’s state-owned Yugoimport-SDPR (49%).

Status-6 (War & Military News) Last night, Ukrainian Air Force carried out strikes on the storage, preparation and launch sites for Russian Shahed/Geran-type long-range OWA-UAVs located on the territory of the Donetsk International Airport in Russia-occupied part of eastern Ukraine. The strikes were reportedly carried out with French-supplied SCALP-EG long-range cruise missiles and US-supplied GBU-39/B SDB guided aerial bombs. The attack was monitored in real-time by a Ukrainian UAV. tweet
